headline: Lam Research Drawn Into Musk’s Terafab AI Chip Ambitions
summary: Lam Research (NasdaqGS:LRCX) has been identified as a supplier engaged by Elon Musk’s Terafab initiative, a new AI chip complex involving Tesla, SpaceX, Intel, and xAI. Terafab is seeking pricing and delivery timelines from leading semiconductor equipment makers, including Lam Research, for advanced chip manufacturing capacity. The project targets cutting edge semiconductor production with applications in AI and space, positioning Lam Research within an early stage fabrication...

headline: The 10 Most Sustainable Companies of 2026. Their Stocks Happen to Be Flying.
summary: Corporate sustainability is under mounting pressure.  On top of the fierce political pushback against sustainability under President Donald Trump, troubles unleashed by tariffs and the Iran war—namely, price spikes and supply chain disruptions—threaten to pry corporate focus away from environmental, social, and governance, or ESG, goals and toward operational challenges.  Executives’ attention is also diverted by the arms race to advance and incorporate artificial intelligence, says Bruno Sarda, Climate Change and Sustainability Services leader at EY Americas.

headline: Musk's staff reaches out to suppliers for Terafab project, Bloomberg News reports
summary: Elon Musk's staff have reached out to chip industry suppliers for his Terafab ‌AI chip complex project with SpaceX and ‌Tesla, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday.  The team has approached companies including ​Applied Materials, Tokyo Electron and Lam Research, and has asked chip manufacturing partner Samsung Electronics Co for support, the report said, citing people familiar with the matter.  Staff ‌have sought price quotes ⁠and delivery times for an array of chipmaking gear, Bloomberg said, adding that in ⁠the past few weeks, they’ve contacted makers of photomasks, substrates, etchers, depositors, cleaning devices, testers and other tools.

raw.headline: Musk's staff reaches out to suppliers for Terafab project, Bloomberg News reports
raw.summary: Elon Musk's staff have reached out to chip industry suppliers for his Terafab ‌AI chip complex project with SpaceX and ‌Tesla, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday.  The team has approached companies including ​Applied Materials, Tokyo Electron and Lam Research, and has asked chip manufacturing partner Samsung Electronics Co for support, the report said, citing people familiar with the matter.  Staff ‌have sought price quotes ⁠and delivery times for an array of chipmaking gear, Bloomberg said, adding that in ⁠the past few weeks, they’ve contacted makers of photomasks, substrates, etchers, depositors, cleaning devices, testers and other tools.
